ogTitle: Point Samson vs Porongurup: Climate Safety

heading: Point Samson vs Porongurup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Samson, Western Australia and Porongurup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Samson has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Porongurup (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Samson or Porongurup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Samson has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Porongurup scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Samson scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Porongurup scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Samson or Porongurup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Samson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
